title: Method and apparatus for non line-of-sight doping
abstract: A method of doping a substrate. The method may include providing a substrate in a process chamber. The substrate may include a semiconductor structure, and a dopant layer disposed on a surface of the semiconductor structure. The method may include maintaining the substrate at a first temperature for a first interval, the first temperature corresponding to a vaporization temperature of the dopant layer. The method may further include rapidly cooling the substrate to a second temperature, less than the first temperature, and heating the substrate from the second temperature to a third temperature, greater than the first temperature.
claims:
Claims Claims  What is claimed is: 1. A method of doping a substrate, comprising: providing a substrate in a process chamber, the substrate comprising a semiconductor structure, and a dopant layer disposed on a surface of the semiconductor structure, wherein the substrate is maintained at a first temperature for a first interval, the first temperature corresponding to a vaporization temperature of the dopant layer; rapidly cooling the substrate to a second temperature, less than the first temperature; and heating the substrate from the second temperature to a third temperature, greater than the first temperature. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the substrate is held at the second temperature for a second interval, wherein the dopant layer forms a non-uniform thickness on the surface of the semiconductor structure. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the substrate is held at the third temperature for a third interval, wherein the dopant layer forms a uniform distribution on the surface of the semiconductor structure. 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein the dopant layer has a first thickness upon a hidden surface of the semiconductor structure before the heating the substrate to the first temperature, and wherein the dopant layer has a second thickness upon the hidden surface 

 of the semiconductor structure after the heating the substrate to the third temperature, the second thickness being greater than the first thickness. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein during the first interval, the process chamber comprises a first dopant partial pressure of the dopant, and wherein during the third interval, a second dopant partial pressure is generated above the substrate, less than the first dopant partial pressure, and wherein a third dopant partial pressure is generated locally adjacent to the semiconductor structure, the third dopant partial pressure being greater than the second dopant partial pressure. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the substrate further comprises a capping layer, disposed over the semiconductor structure and the dopant layer. 7. The method of claim 3, the method further comprising rapidly cooling the substrate after the third interval. 8. The method of claim7, wherein the substrate is disposed in contact with a substrate holder in the process chamber, wherein the substrate is disposed at a first position, having a first thermal contact with the substrate holder and is heated by a substrate heater during the third interval, the rapidly cooling comprising: moving the substrate to a second position, wherein the substrate is in a second thermal contact with the substrate holder, greater than the first thermal contact; and turning off the substrate heater. 9. An apparatus for doping a substrate, comprising: 

 a process chamber, the process chamber comprising an interior wall; a substrate holder, disposed within the process chamber; a substrate heater assembly, disposed opposite the substrate holder, outside the process chamber; and a controller, coupled to substrate heater assembly, and configured to: send a set of control signals to the substrate heater assembly and the substrate holder to cycle the substrate between a first substrate temperature and a second substrate temperature, wherein the substrate heater assembly is switched between an ON and OFF state and the substrate holder is simultaneously switched between a low thermal conductivity configuration and a high thermal conductivity configuration. 10. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the substrate heater assembly comprises a lamp assembly, the apparatus further comprising a process chamber window, disposed between the lamp assembly and the substrate holder. 11. The apparatus of claim 9, further comprising a gas transport assembly, coupled to the substrate holder, wherein the controller is configured to send a gas control signal to conduct gas to the substrate holder to establish the high thermal conductivity configuration. 12. The apparatus of claim 9, the substrate holder further comprising a lift assembly, wherein the controller is configured to send a lift control signal to move the lift assembly to establish the high thermal conductivity configuration. 13. The apparatus of claim 9, further comprising an plasma generation assembly, disposed around the process chamber, to generate a plasma comprising dopant species. 14. A method of doping a substrate, comprising: providing a substrate in a process chamber, the substrate comprising a semiconductor structure; providing a partial pressure of a condensing dopant species in the process chamber; heating the substrate to a first temperature for a first interval, the first temperature corresponding to a vaporization temperature of the condensing dopant species; rapidly cooling the substrate to a second temperature, less than the first temperature; maintaining the substrate at the second temperature for a second interval; and heating the substrate from the second temperature to a third temperature, greater than the first temperature, and maintaining the substrate at the third temperature for a third interval. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the first temperature is 175 Â°C to 225 Â°C, wherein the second temperature is 130 Â°C to 170 Â°C, and wherein the third temperature is 250 Â°C to 400 Â°C.
